prompt: create a counter using 7 segment display. every time user hit button, the number increse, after 9 goes back to 0

trace.text:
Your button counter is ready! 🎉 Here's a full breakdown:

---

## 📌 Wiring Table

| Arduino Pin | Component | Connection |
|---|---|---|
| D2 | R_A → Segment A | Digital OUT |
| D3 | R_B → Segment B | Digital OUT |
| D4 | R_C → Segment C | Digital OUT |
| D5 | R_D → Segment D | Digital OUT |
| D6 | R_E → Segment E | Digital OUT |
| D7 | R_F → Segment F | Digital OUT |
| D8 | R_G → Segment G | Digital OUT |
| GND | Display (common cathode) | Common pin |
| D9 | Button (one leg) | INPUT_PULLUP |
| GND | Button (other leg) | Ground |

Each segment is protected by its own **220Ω resistor** in series.

---

## 💡 How It Works

- The button uses the Arduino's **internal pull-up**, so no extra resistor is needed — it reads HIGH at rest, LOW when pressed.
- On each **button press** (falling edge detected), the counter increments.
- After **9**, it wraps back to **0**.
- A **50ms debounce delay** prevents false multiple-counts from button bounce.
- Digits use an **if/else lookup** for segments A–G (common cathode display).

---

## ⚡ Power Budget

| Item | Current (approx) |
|---|---|
| Each lit segment @ 5V, 220Ω | ~14mA |
| Max segments ON (digit 8 = all 7) | ~98mA |
| Button pin (INPUT_PULLUP) | < 1mA |
| **Total max draw** | **~100mA** |

> ✅ Well within the Arduino Uno's USB supply limit (~500mA). Each individual pin stays under the 40mA absolute maximum (14mA per segment). Safe to run!

issues: BTN (button) has Arduino signal wires on both sides. Use one side for input and the opposite side for power/ground reference.
